Perfect! I have gathered comprehensive information about the UI components in the T3 Code project. Let me compile the findings into a complete report.

## Comprehensive UI Components Analysis - T3 Code Project

Based on my exploration of `apps/web/src`, here's the complete breakdown:

### 1. STYLING APPROACH: Tailwind CSS with CSS Variables

**File: `/apps/web/src/index.css`**

The project uses:
- **Tailwind CSS** as the primary styling framework
- **CSS custom properties (variables)** for theming
- **Color palette with semantic tokens** (primary, secondary, destructive, success, warning, info, etc.)
- **Dark mode support** via `.dark` class on `html` element with `@variant dark` CSS
- **Light and dark color schemes** defined at `:root` with separate dark variant

Key theme colors include:
- Primary color: oklch-based purple (oklch(0.488 0.217 264) light, oklch(0.588 0.217 264) dark)
- Destructive: red-500/red-700
- Success: emerald
- Warning: amber
- Info: blue
- Border/Input/Muted using alpha-based colors with `--alpha()` function

### 2. EXISTING SELECTOR/DROPDOWN/MODAL COMPONENTS (Perfect for Theme Selector)

#### **A. Select Component** (`/apps/web/src/components/ui/select.tsx`)
- Uses `@base-ui/react/select` primitives
- Supports multiple size variants (default, lg, sm)
- Supports variant styles (default, ghost)
- Has SelectButton, SelectTrigger, SelectValue, SelectPopup, SelectItem, SelectSeparator, SelectGroup, SelectGroupLabel
- Auto-positioning with side/align/sideOffset
- **IDEAL for theme selector if simple**

#### **B. Combobox Component** (`/apps/web/src/components/ui/combobox.tsx`)
- Uses `@base-ui/react/combobox` primitives
- Supports searchable lists
- Has multiple, chips support
- ComboboxInput, ComboboxPopup, ComboboxItem, ComboboxList, etc.
- **Better for complex selections with search**

#### **C. Menu Component** (`/apps/web/src/components/ui/menu.tsx`)
- Uses `@base-ui/react/menu` primitives
- MenuItem, MenuCheckboxItem, MenuRadioItem support
- MenuGroup, MenuSeparator, MenuShortcut
- **EXCELLENT for dropdown menus with radio/checkbox items**
- Already used in PlanSidebar for ellipsis menu

#### **D. Radio Group Component** (`/apps/web/src/components/ui/radio-group.tsx`)
- Uses `@base-ui/react/radio-group` primitives
- Radio component with single selection
- Simple, compact styling with focus states

#### **E. Toggle/Toggle Group** (`/apps/web/src/components/ui/toggle-group.tsx`)
- Uses `@base-ui/react/toggle-group` primitives
- Supports horizontal/vertical orientation
- Can be used for mode selection (Light/Dark/System)
- **Ideal for theme mode selector UI**

#### **F. Switch Component** (`/apps/web/src/components/ui/switch.tsx`)
- Uses `@base-ui/react/switch` primitives
- Simple on/off toggle
- **Could work for theme toggle if just Light/Dark**

#### **G. Dialog Component** (`/apps/web/src/components/ui/dialog.tsx`)
- Uses `@base-ui/react/dialog` primitives
- DialogPopup, DialogHeader, DialogFooter, DialogTitle, DialogDescription, DialogPanel
- Mobile-aware (bottom sheet on mobile)
- **For settings panels/modals**

#### **H. Popover Component** (`/apps/web/src/components/ui/popover.tsx`)
- Uses `@base-ui/react/popover` primitives
- PopoverTrigger, PopoverPopup, PopoverTitle, PopoverDescription
- Lightweight alternative to Dialog
- **Good for quick theme selection dropdown**

### 3. EXISTING THEME MANAGEMENT SYSTEM

**File: `/apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`**

Complete theme hook already exists:
```typescript
export function useTheme() {
  // Returns: { theme: 'light' | 'dark' | 'system', setTheme, resolvedTheme }
  // Storage: localStorage key 't3code:theme'
  // System detection: matchMedia("(prefers-color-scheme: dark)")
  // DOM update: Toggles .dark class on document.documentElement
  // Transitions: Supports suppressTransitions flag
}
```

**Key features:**
- Supports three modes: `'light' | 'dark' | 'system'`
- Persists to localStorage
- Listens to system preference changes
- Listens to storage changes across tabs (sync between windows)
- Prevents visual transitions during theme change using `no-transitions` class

### 4. LAYOUT STRUCTURE

#### **Main Sidebar** (`/apps/web/src/components/Sidebar.tsx` - 200+ lines)
- Uses `SidebarProvider` context from `ui/sidebar.tsx`
- Sidebar header with project navigation
- Thread list with status indicators
- Collapsible groups for organization
- Custom scrollbar styling
- Desktop update notifications
- Settings button with SettingsIcon

#### **Plan Sidebar** (`/apps/web/src/components/PlanSidebar.tsx`)
- Right-side panel for plan display
- Header with Badge and action menu (ellipsis)
- Uses Menu component for actions (Copy, Download, Save)
- Collapsible sections
- Status indicators with colored backgrounds

#### **Branch Toolbar** (`/apps/web/src/components/BranchToolbar.tsx`)
- Top toolbar between composer and editor
- Environment mode selector (Local/Worktree)
- Branch selector via BranchToolbarBranchSelector
- Button with environment toggle

#### **Chat View** (`/apps/web/src/components/ChatView.tsx` - 2000+ lines)
- Main editor interface
- Composer prompt editor at bottom
- Timeline of chat turns/messages
- Markdown rendering
- Turn controls (accept/retry/compare)
- Uses extensive UI components

#### **Terminal Drawer** (`/apps/web/src/components/ThreadTerminalDrawer.tsx`)
- Bottom drawer with terminal output
- Uses xterm for terminal emulation
- Custom scrollbar styling to match app

#### **Diff Panel** (`/apps/web/src/components/DiffPanel.tsx`)
- File diff visualization
- Stacked vs Split view toggle using ToggleGroup
- Uses `@pierre/diffs/react` for rendering
- Custom CSS variables for theme integration

### 5. COMMON UI COMPONENTS

**Base Components Available:**
- Button (multiple variants: default, destructive, outline, ghost, secondary, link; sizes: default, sm, lg, xs, icon variants)
- Input (multiple sizes: sm, default, lg)
- Label
- Badge
- Card
- Alert/AlertDialog
- Checkbox
- Collapsible
- Command
- Empty state
- Field/Fieldset
- Form
- Group
- Input-group
- Kbd
- Separator
- Sheet (mobile drawer)
- Skeleton
- Spinner
- Textarea
- Toast (with toast.logic.ts)

### 6. RECOMMENDED APPROACH FOR THEME SELECTOR

**Option 1: Dropdown Menu (Recommended)**
```
Location: Header/Top bar or Settings
Component: Menu with MenuRadioItem (or MenuCheckboxItem for "System")
Items:
  - Light
  - Dark
  - System (with checkmark indicator)
```

**Option 2: Toggle Group**
```
Location: Sidebar header or Settings
Component: ToggleGroup with horizontal orientation
Items:
  - Light (icon)
  - Dark (icon)
  - System (icon)
Single selection with visual feedback
```

**Option 3: Settings Panel**
```
Location: Settings modal/dialog
Component: RadioGroup or Toggle Group with descriptions
Items with labels explaining each option
```

### 7. EXAMPLES OF EXISTING MENUS

**In PlanSidebar:**
```typescript
<Menu>
  <MenuTrigger render={<Button size="icon-xs" variant="ghost" />}>
    <EllipsisIcon className="size-3.5" />
  </MenuTrigger>
  <MenuPopup align="end">
    <MenuItem onClick={handleCopyPlan}>Copy to clipboard</MenuItem>
    <MenuItem onClick={handleDownload}>Download as markdown</MenuItem>
    <MenuItem onClick={handleSaveToWorkspace}>Save to workspace</MenuItem>
  </MenuPopup>
</Menu>
```

### 8. CUSTOM STYLING NOTES

- All components use `cn()` utility (from `~/lib/utils` - likely clsx/classnames with Tailwind merge)
- Components support `className` prop for customization
- Extensive use of Tailwind's complex selectors (has:, focus-visible:, data-*, etc.)
- Custom CSS for app-specific elements (chat markdown, terminal scrollbars, drag regions)
- No CSS modules - pure Tailwind approach

### Key Files Summary

| File | Purpose | Type |
|------|---------|------|
| `/index.css` | Global styles, Tailwind config, theme variables | CSS |
| `/hooks/useTheme.ts` | Theme management hook | Logic |
| `/components/Sidebar.tsx` | Main left sidebar | Component |
| `/components/PlanSidebar.tsx` | Right plan panel | Component |
| `/components/ChatView.tsx` | Main editor area | Component |
| `/components/ui/select.tsx` | Select dropdown | Base UI |
| `/components/ui/menu.tsx` | Menu component | Base UI |
| `/components/ui/toggle-group.tsx` | Toggle group | Base UI |
| `/components/ui/dialog.tsx` | Modal dialog | Base UI |
| `/components/ui/button.tsx` | Button component | Base UI |

All existing components are fully read and documented above with their complete implementations.

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the monorepo structure. Let me compile this into a detailed report.

## Comprehensive T3 Code Monorepo Packages Exploration Report

Based on my thorough exploration of the T3 Code monorepo, here's everything you need to understand about the `packages/shared` and `packages/contracts` packages.

---

### 1. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

#### **packages/shared**
```
packages/shared/
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── src/
│   ├── model.ts           - Model option utilities and resolution functions
│   ├── git.ts             - Git branch name sanitization utilities
│   ├── logging.ts         - Rotating file sink for log management
│   ├── shell.ts           - Shell environment utilities (PATH extraction)
│   ├── Net.ts             - Network utilities (port availability checking)
│   ├── model.test.ts
│   ├── git.test.ts
│   ├── shell.test.ts
│   └── Net.test.ts
```

#### **packages/contracts**
```
packages/contracts/
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts                    - Main exports file (aggregates all schemas)
│   ├── baseSchemas.ts              - Reusable Effect.Schema definitions
│   ├── model.ts                    - Model definitions and constants
│   ├── provider.ts                 - Provider session and input/output schemas
│   ├── providerRuntime.ts          - Provider runtime contracts (large file)
│   ├── orchestration.ts            - Core orchestration contracts (large file)
│   ├── terminal.ts                 - Terminal interaction schemas
│   ├── git.ts                      - Git operations schemas
│   ├── ipc.ts                      - IPC bridge interfaces (Desktop)
│   ├── ws.ts                       - WebSocket RPC method/channel definitions
│   ├── server.ts                   - Server configuration and response schemas
│   ├── keybindings.ts              - Keybinding command and rule schemas
│   ├── editor.ts                   - Editor selection schemas
│   ├── project.ts                  - Project file search/write schemas
│   ├── [all test files]
```

---

### 2. SUBPATH EXPORTS DEFINITION (packages/shared/package.json)

```json
"exports": {
  "./model": {
    "types": "./src/model.ts",
    "import": "./src/model.ts"
  },
  "./git": {
    "types": "./src/git.ts",
    "import": "./src/git.ts"
  },
  "./logging": {
    "types": "./src/logging.ts",
    "import": "./src/logging.ts"
  },
  "./shell": {
    "types": "./src/shell.ts",
    "import": "./src/shell.ts"
  },
  "./Net": {
    "types": "./src/Net.ts",
    "import": "./src/Net.ts"
  }
}
```

**Key Pattern**: Direct TypeScript source exports (not compiled). Each subpath maps directly to a single source file. Note that `./Net` is capitalized while others are lowercase.

---

### 3. EXISTING TYPE DEFINITIONS AND SCHEMAS IN packages/contracts

The package uses **Effect.Schema** for all type definitions, providing both runtime validation and type safety.

#### **Base Schemas (baseSchemas.ts)**
- **String Refinements**: `TrimmedString`, `TrimmedNonEmptyString`
- **Number Refinements**: `NonNegativeInt`, `PositiveInt`
- **Branded IDs**: `ThreadId`, `ProjectId`, `CommandId`, `EventId`, `MessageId`, `TurnId`, `ProviderItemId`, `RuntimeSessionId`, `RuntimeItemId`, `RuntimeRequestId`, `RuntimeTaskId`, `ApprovalRequestId`, `CheckpointRef`
- **Datetime**: `IsoDateTime`

#### **Model Types (model.ts)**
- `CODEX_REASONING_EFFORT_OPTIONS`: `["xhigh", "high", "medium", "low"]`
- `CodexReasoningEffort`: Union type derived from above
- `MODEL_OPTIONS_BY_PROVIDER`: Dictionary mapping provider to available models
- `ModelSlug`: Union of built-in slugs plus custom string type
- `DEFAULT_MODEL_BY_PROVIDER`: Maps provider to default model
- `MODEL_SLUG_ALIASES_BY_PROVIDER`: Maps aliases to canonical model names

#### **Orchestration Types (orchestration.ts - Large File ~36KB)**
- `ProviderKind`: `"codex"`
- `ProviderApprovalPolicy`: `"untrusted" | "on-failure" | "on-request" | "never"`
- `ProviderSandboxMode`: `"read-only" | "workspace-write" | "danger-full-access"`
- `ProviderServiceTier`: `"fast" | "flex"`
- `RuntimeMode`: `"approval-required" | "full-access"`
- `ProviderInteractionMode`: `"default" | "plan"`
- `ProviderRequestKind`: `"command" | "file-read" | "file-change"`
- `ChatImageAttachment`, `UploadChatImageAttachment`: Image attachment schemas
- `ChatAttachment`: Union of image attachments
- WS method/channel constants
- Constants: `PROVIDER_SEND_TURN_MAX_INPUT_CHARS` (120,000), `PROVIDER_SEND_TURN_MAX_ATTACHMENTS` (8)

#### **Provider Types (provider.ts)**
- `ProviderSession`: Session state schema
- `ProviderSessionStartInput`: Input for starting a session
- `ProviderSendTurnInput`: Input for sending a turn/message
- `ProviderTurnStartResult`: Result from turn start
- `ProviderEvent`: Unified event schema

#### **Terminal Types (terminal.ts)**
- `TerminalSessionSnapshot`: Complete state of a terminal session
- `TerminalOpenInput`, `TerminalWriteInput`, `TerminalResizeInput`, `TerminalCloseInput`
- `TerminalSessionStatus`: `"starting" | "running" | "exited" | "error"`
- `TerminalEvent`: Discriminated union of terminal events

#### **Git Types (git.ts)**
- `GitStackedAction`: `"commit" | "commit_push" | "commit_push_pr"`
- `GitBranch`: Schema with name, remote info, current flag, worktree path
- Input schemas: `GitStatusInput`, `GitPullInput`, `GitRunStackedActionInput`, `GitCreateWorktreeInput`, etc.
- Result schemas: `GitStatusResult`, `GitListBranchesResult`

#### **Keybinding Types (keybindings.ts)**
- `KeybindingCommand`: Union of static commands or script patterns
- `KeybindingRule`: Key binding configuration
- `KeybindingShortcut`: Parsed keyboard shortcut
- `KeybindingWhenNode`: Recursive AST for "when" conditions
- Constants: `MAX_KEYBINDING_VALUE_LENGTH` (64), `MAX_WHEN_EXPRESSION_DEPTH` (64), `MAX_KEYBINDINGS_COUNT` (256)

#### **Server Types (server.ts)**
- `ServerConfig`: Complete server configuration
- `ServerProviderStatus`: Provider health status
- `ServerConfigIssue`: Configuration problems

#### **Editor & Project Types**
- `EditorId`: Literals `"cursor" | "vscode" | "zed" | "file-manager"`
- `ProjectSearchEntriesInput/Result`: File/directory search
- `ProjectWriteFileInput/Result`: File write operations

#### **WebSocket/IPC Types (ws.ts, ipc.ts)**
- `WS_METHODS`: Constant dictionary of RPC method names
- `WS_CHANNELS`: Constant dictionary of push channels
- `DesktopBridge`: Desktop app bridge interface
- `NativeApi`: Type-safe API for native calls

---

### 4. PATTERNS USED IN packages/shared

#### **Module Export Pattern**
Each module exports a cohesive set of utilities:

**model.ts** - Model Resolution Utilities:
```typescript
export function getModelOptions(provider: ProviderKind)
export function getDefaultModel(provider: ProviderKind): ModelSlug
export function normalizeModelSlug(model: string | null | undefined, provider: ProviderKind): ModelSlug | null
export function resolveModelSlug(model: string | null | undefined, provider: ProviderKind): ModelSlug
export function resolveModelSlugForProvider(provider: ProviderKind, model: string | null | undefined): ModelSlug
export function getReasoningEffortOptions(provider: ProviderKind): ReadonlyArray<CodexReasoningEffort>
export function getDefaultReasoningEffort(provider: ProviderKind): CodexReasoningEffort | null
export { CODEX_REASONING_EFFORT_OPTIONS }  // Re-export from contracts
```

**git.ts** - Git Utilities:
```typescript
export function sanitizeBranchFragment(raw: string): string
export function sanitizeFeatureBranchName(raw: string): string
export function resolveAutoFeatureBranchName(
  existingBranchNames: readonly string[],
  preferredBranch?: string
): string
```

**logging.ts** - Rotating Log File Sink:
```typescript
export interface RotatingFileSinkOptions { /* ... */ }
export class RotatingFileSink { 
  constructor(options: RotatingFileSinkOptions)
  write(chunk: string | Buffer): void
  private rotate(): void
  // ... more methods
}
```

**shell.ts** - Shell Environment:
```typescript
export function extractPathFromShellOutput(output: string): string | null
export function readPathFromLoginShell(shell: string, execFile?: ExecFileSyncLike): string | undefined
```

**Net.ts** - Network Utilities (Effect-based):
```typescript
export class NetError extends Data.TaggedError("NetError")
export interface NetServiceShape {
  readonly canListenOnHost: (port: number, host: string) => Effect.Effect<boolean>
  readonly isPortAvailableOnLoopback: (port: number) => Effect.Effect<boolean>
  readonly reserveLoopbackPort: (host?: string) => Effect.Effect<number, NetError>
  readonly findAvailablePort: (preferred: number) => Effect.Effect<number, NetError>
}
export class NetService extends ServiceMap.Service<NetService, NetServiceShape>()
```

#### **Key Characteristics:**
- **Single responsibility**: Each module focuses on one domain
- **Pure functions**: Most utilities are pure/deterministic
- **Effect-based**: Net.ts uses Effect for resource management
- **Reexports from contracts**: Imports types from contracts, re-exports some constants
- **No external dependencies** beyond Effect and Node.js built-ins

---

### 5. HOW apps/web IMPORTS FROM THESE PACKAGES

#### **From @t3tools/contracts (Direct Imports)**

**Type Imports:**
```typescript
import { ThreadId, ProjectId, TurnId, MessageId, EventId } from "@t3tools/contracts"
import type { ProviderKind, ProviderServiceTier } from "@t3tools/contracts"
import type { OrchestrationReadModel, OrchestrationSessionStatus } from "@t3tools/contracts"
import type { GitBranch, GitStatusResult, GitStackedAction } from "@t3tools/contracts"
import type { TerminalEvent, TerminalSessionSnapshot } from "@t3tools/contracts"
import type { NativeApi, DesktopBridge, ContextMenuItem } from "@t3tools/contracts"
import type { KeybindingCommand, KeybindingShortcut, ResolvedKeybindingsConfig } from "@t3tools/contracts"
import { EDITORS, EditorId } from "@t3tools/contracts"
import type { UserInputQuestion } from "@t3tools/contracts"
```

**Value Imports (Constants/Enums):**
```typescript

import { EDITORS, type EditorId } from "@t3tools/contracts"
import { MAX_KEYBINDING_VALUE_LENGTH, type KeybindingCommand } from "@t3tools/contracts"
```

#### **From @t3tools/shared/model (Subpath Imports)**

```typescript

  getModelOptions,
  normalizeModelSlug,
  resolveModelSlug,
  resolveModelSlugForProvider,
  getDefaultModel,
  getReasoningEffortOptions,
  getDefaultReasoningEffort
} from "@t3tools/shared/model"
```

#### **Usage Examples in apps/web:**

**store.ts** - State management:
```typescript
import { DEFAULT_MODEL_BY_PROVIDER, type ProviderKind } from "@t3tools/contracts"

// Used for model dropdown options, normalization, and defaults
```

**appSettings.ts** - App settings:
```typescript

// Used for service tier options and model validation
```

**keybindings.ts** - Keyboard handling:
```typescript

// Used for shortcut matching and command resolution
```

**session-logic.ts** - Session orchestration:
```typescript

// Used for event handling and thread state management
```

#### **Import Patterns Across 40+ Files in apps/web:**
- **~50 files import from `@t3tools/contracts`** (types, IDs, constants)
- **~3-4 files import from `@t3tools/shared/model`** (utility functions)
- **No imports** from `/git`, `/logging`, `/shell`, or `/Net` modules in web (those are server/backend utilities)
- **Branded ID types** (`ThreadId`, `ProjectId`, etc.) are imported in utility files like `lib/utils.ts` for type safety

---

### 6. KEY INSIGHTS & ARCHITECTURAL PATTERNS

**Type Safety Strategy:**
- Contracts package: All runtime-validated schemas using Effect.Schema
- Shared package: Pure utility functions that operate on contract types
- Web app: Imports types for compile-time safety, imports utilities for data transformation

**Separation of Concerns:**
- **contracts**: Domain types, RPC schemas, constants, interfaces
- **shared**: Pure algorithms and utilities (model resolution, git branch naming, logging, networking)
- **web**: UI logic, state management, component behavior

**Export Strategy:**
- contracts: Single default export (index.ts re-exports everything)
- shared: Multiple named subpath exports (no default export)

**Dependency Flow:**
- web → shared (utilities) → contracts (types)
- web → contracts (types and constants) → Effect

---

### Summary Files & Paths

**packages/shared:**
- `/packages/shared/package.json`
- `/packages/shared/src/model.ts` (79 lines)
- `/packages/shared/src/git.ts` (62 lines)
- `/packages/shared/src/logging.ts` (116 lines)
- `/packages/shared/src/shell.ts` (39 lines)
- `/packages/shared/src/Net.ts` (181 lines)

**packages/contracts:**
- `/packages/contracts/package.json`

- `/packages/contracts/src/baseSchemas.ts` (branded IDs)
- `/packages/contracts/src/model.ts` (model definitions)
- `/packages/contracts/src/orchestration.ts` (36KB - main domain logic)
- `/packages/contracts/src/provider.ts` (provider contracts)
- `/packages/contracts/src/providerRuntime.ts` (runtime contracts)
- Plus: terminal, git, ipc, ws, server, keybindings, editor, project schemas

This structure enables type-safe, composable interactions across the monorepo while maintaining clear separation between domain contracts and utility implementations.

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the monorepo structure. Let me compile this into a detailed report.

## Comprehensive T3 Code Monorepo Packages Exploration Report

Based on my thorough exploration of the T3 Code monorepo, here's everything you need to understand about the `packages/shared` and `packages/contracts` packages.

---

### 1. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

#### **packages/shared**
```
packages/shared/
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── src/
│   ├── model.ts           - Model option utilities and resolution functions
│   ├── git.ts             - Git branch name sanitization utilities
│   ├── logging.ts         - Rotating file sink for log management
│   ├── shell.ts           - Shell environment utilities (PATH extraction)
│   ├── Net.ts             - Network utilities (port availability checking)
│   ├── model.test.ts
│   ├── git.test.ts
│   ├── shell.test.ts
│   └── Net.test.ts
```

#### **packages/contracts**
```
packages/contracts/
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts                    - Main exports file (aggregates all schemas)
│   ├── baseSchemas.ts              - Reusable Effect.Schema definitions
│   ├── model.ts                    - Model definitions and constants
│   ├── provider.ts                 - Provider session and input/output schemas
│   ├── providerRuntime.ts          - Provider runtime contracts (large file)
│   ├── orchestration.ts            - Core orchestration contracts (large file)
│   ├── terminal.ts                 - Terminal interaction schemas
│   ├── git.ts                      - Git operations schemas
│   ├── ipc.ts                      - IPC bridge interfaces (Desktop)
│   ├── ws.ts                       - WebSocket RPC method/channel definitions
│   ├── server.ts                   - Server configuration and response schemas
│   ├── keybindings.ts              - Keybinding command and rule schemas
│   ├── editor.ts                   - Editor selection schemas
│   ├── project.ts                  - Project file search/write schemas
│   ├── [all test files]
```

---

### 2. SUBPATH EXPORTS DEFINITION (packages/shared/package.json)

```json
"exports": {
  "./model": {
    "types": "./src/model.ts",
    "import": "./src/model.ts"
  },
  "./git": {
    "types": "./src/git.ts",
    "import": "./src/git.ts"
  },
  "./logging": {
    "types": "./src/logging.ts",
    "import": "./src/logging.ts"
  },
  "./shell": {
    "types": "./src/shell.ts",
    "import": "./src/shell.ts"
  },
  "./Net": {
    "types": "./src/Net.ts",
    "import": "./src/Net.ts"
  }
}
```

**Key Pattern**: Direct TypeScript source exports (not compiled). Each subpath maps directly to a single source file. Note that `./Net` is capitalized while others are lowercase.

---

### 3. EXISTING TYPE DEFINITIONS AND SCHEMAS IN packages/contracts

The package uses **Effect.Schema** for all type definitions, providing both runtime validation and type safety.

#### **Base Schemas (baseSchemas.ts)**
- **String Refinements**: `TrimmedString`, `TrimmedNonEmptyString`
- **Number Refinements**: `NonNegativeInt`, `PositiveInt`
- **Branded IDs**: `ThreadId`, `ProjectId`, `CommandId`, `EventId`, `MessageId`, `TurnId`, `ProviderItemId`, `RuntimeSessionId`, `RuntimeItemId`, `RuntimeRequestId`, `RuntimeTaskId`, `ApprovalRequestId`, `CheckpointRef`
- **Datetime**: `IsoDateTime`

#### **Model Types (model.ts)**
- `CODEX_REASONING_EFFORT_OPTIONS`: `["xhigh", "high", "medium", "low"]`
- `CodexReasoningEffort`: Union type derived from above
- `MODEL_OPTIONS_BY_PROVIDER`: Dictionary mapping provider to available models
- `ModelSlug`: Union of built-in slugs plus custom string type
- `DEFAULT_MODEL_BY_PROVIDER`: Maps provider to default model
- `MODEL_SLUG_ALIASES_BY_PROVIDER`: Maps aliases to canonical model names

#### **Orchestration Types (orchestration.ts - Large File ~36KB)**
- `ProviderKind`: `"codex"`
- `ProviderApprovalPolicy`: `"untrusted" | "on-failure" | "on-request" | "never"`
- `ProviderSandboxMode`: `"read-only" | "workspace-write" | "danger-full-access"`
- `ProviderServiceTier`: `"fast" | "flex"`
- `RuntimeMode`: `"approval-required" | "full-access"`
- `ProviderInteractionMode`: `"default" | "plan"`
- `ProviderRequestKind`: `"command" | "file-read" | "file-change"`
- `ChatImageAttachment`, `UploadChatImageAttachment`: Image attachment schemas
- `ChatAttachment`: Union of image attachments
- WS method/channel constants
- Constants: `PROVIDER_SEND_TURN_MAX_INPUT_CHARS` (120,000), `PROVIDER_SEND_TURN_MAX_ATTACHMENTS` (8)

#### **Provider Types (provider.ts)**
- `ProviderSession`: Session state schema
- `ProviderSessionStartInput`: Input for starting a session
- `ProviderSendTurnInput`: Input for sending a turn/message
- `ProviderTurnStartResult`: Result from turn start
- `ProviderEvent`: Unified event schema

#### **Terminal Types (terminal.ts)**
- `TerminalSessionSnapshot`: Complete state of a terminal session
- `TerminalOpenInput`, `TerminalWriteInput`, `TerminalResizeInput`, `TerminalCloseInput`
- `TerminalSessionStatus`: `"starting" | "running" | "exited" | "error"`
- `TerminalEvent`: Discriminated union of terminal events

#### **Git Types (git.ts)**
- `GitStackedAction`: `"commit" | "commit_push" | "commit_push_pr"`
- `GitBranch`: Schema with name, remote info, current flag, worktree path
- Input schemas: `GitStatusInput`, `GitPullInput`, `GitRunStackedActionInput`, `GitCreateWorktreeInput`, etc.
- Result schemas: `GitStatusResult`, `GitListBranchesResult`

#### **Keybinding Types (keybindings.ts)**
- `KeybindingCommand`: Union of static commands or script patterns
- `KeybindingRule`: Key binding configuration
- `KeybindingShortcut`: Parsed keyboard shortcut
- `KeybindingWhenNode`: Recursive AST for "when" conditions
- Constants: `MAX_KEYBINDING_VALUE_LENGTH` (64), `MAX_WHEN_EXPRESSION_DEPTH` (64), `MAX_KEYBINDINGS_COUNT` (256)

#### **Server Types (server.ts)**
- `ServerConfig`: Complete server configuration
- `ServerProviderStatus`: Provider health status
- `ServerConfigIssue`: Configuration problems

#### **Editor & Project Types**
- `EditorId`: Literals `"cursor" | "vscode" | "zed" | "file-manager"`
- `ProjectSearchEntriesInput/Result`: File/directory search
- `ProjectWriteFileInput/Result`: File write operations

#### **WebSocket/IPC Types (ws.ts, ipc.ts)**
- `WS_METHODS`: Constant dictionary of RPC method names
- `WS_CHANNELS`: Constant dictionary of push channels
- `DesktopBridge`: Desktop app bridge interface
- `NativeApi`: Type-safe API for native calls

---

### 4. PATTERNS USED IN packages/shared

#### **Module Export Pattern**
Each module exports a cohesive set of utilities:

**model.ts** - Model Resolution Utilities:
```typescript
export function getModelOptions(provider: ProviderKind)
export function getDefaultModel(provider: ProviderKind): ModelSlug
export function normalizeModelSlug(model: string | null | undefined, provider: ProviderKind): ModelSlug | null
export function resolveModelSlug(model: string | null | undefined, provider: ProviderKind): ModelSlug
export function resolveModelSlugForProvider(provider: ProviderKind, model: string | null | undefined): ModelSlug
export function getReasoningEffortOptions(provider: ProviderKind): ReadonlyArray<CodexReasoningEffort>
export function getDefaultReasoningEffort(provider: ProviderKind): CodexReasoningEffort | null
export { CODEX_REASONING_EFFORT_OPTIONS }  // Re-export from contracts
```

**git.ts** - Git Utilities:
```typescript
export function sanitizeBranchFragment(raw: string): string
export function sanitizeFeatureBranchName(raw: string): string
export function resolveAutoFeatureBranchName(
  existingBranchNames: readonly string[],
  preferredBranch?: string
): string
```

**logging.ts** - Rotating Log File Sink:
```typescript
export interface RotatingFileSinkOptions { /* ... */ }
export class RotatingFileSink { 
  constructor(options: RotatingFileSinkOptions)
  write(chunk: string | Buffer): void
  private rotate(): void
  // ... more methods
}
```

**shell.ts** - Shell Environment:
```typescript
export function extractPathFromShellOutput(output: string): string | null
export function readPathFromLoginShell(shell: string, execFile?: ExecFileSyncLike): string | undefined
```

**Net.ts** - Network Utilities (Effect-based):
```typescript
export class NetError extends Data.TaggedError("NetError")
export interface NetServiceShape {
  readonly canListenOnHost: (port: number, host: string) => Effect.Effect<boolean>
  readonly isPortAvailableOnLoopback: (port: number) => Effect.Effect<boolean>
  readonly reserveLoopbackPort: (host?: string) => Effect.Effect<number, NetError>
  readonly findAvailablePort: (preferred: number) => Effect.Effect<number, NetError>
}
export class NetService extends ServiceMap.Service<NetService, NetServiceShape>()
```

#### **Key Characteristics:**
- **Single responsibility**: Each module focuses on one domain
- **Pure functions**: Most utilities are pure/deterministic
- **Effect-based**: Net.ts uses Effect for resource management
- **Reexports from contracts**: Imports types from contracts, re-exports some constants
- **No external dependencies** beyond Effect and Node.js built-ins

---

### 5. HOW apps/web IMPORTS FROM THESE PACKAGES

#### **From @t3tools/contracts (Direct Imports)**

**Type Imports:**
```typescript
import { ThreadId, ProjectId, TurnId, MessageId, EventId } from "@t3tools/contracts"
import type { ProviderKind, ProviderServiceTier } from "@t3tools/contracts"
import type { OrchestrationReadModel, OrchestrationSessionStatus } from "@t3tools/contracts"
import type { GitBranch, GitStatusResult, GitStackedAction } from "@t3tools/contracts"
import type { TerminalEvent, TerminalSessionSnapshot } from "@t3tools/contracts"
import type { NativeApi, DesktopBridge, ContextMenuItem } from "@t3tools/contracts"
import type { KeybindingCommand, KeybindingShortcut, ResolvedKeybindingsConfig } from "@t3tools/contracts"
import { EDITORS, EditorId } from "@t3tools/contracts"
import type { UserInputQuestion } from "@t3tools/contracts"
```

**Value Imports (Constants/Enums):**
```typescript
import { DEFAULT_MODEL_BY_PROVIDER, ThreadId } from "@t3tools/contracts"
import { CommandId, MessageId, ProjectId, ThreadId } from "@t3tools/contracts"
import { EDITORS, type EditorId } from "@t3tools/contracts"
import { MAX_KEYBINDING_VALUE_LENGTH, type KeybindingCommand } from "@t3tools/contracts"
```

#### **From @t3tools/shared/model (Subpath Imports)**

```typescript
import { 
  getModelOptions,
  normalizeModelSlug,
  resolveModelSlug,
  resolveModelSlugForProvider,
  getDefaultModel,
  getReasoningEffortOptions,
  getDefaultReasoningEffort
} from "@t3tools/shared/model"
```

#### **Usage Examples in apps/web:**

**store.ts** - State management:
```typescript
import { DEFAULT_MODEL_BY_PROVIDER, type ProviderKind } from "@t3tools/contracts"
import { getModelOptions, normalizeModelSlug, resolveModelSlug } from "@t3tools/shared/model"

// Used for model dropdown options, normalization, and defaults
```

**appSettings.ts** - App settings:
```typescript
import { type ProviderKind, type ProviderServiceTier } from "@t3tools/contracts"
import { getDefaultModel, getModelOptions, normalizeModelSlug } from "@t3tools/shared/model"

// Used for service tier options and model validation
```

**keybindings.ts** - Keyboard handling:
```typescript
import { type KeybindingCommand, type KeybindingShortcut } from "@t3tools/contracts"
import { type ResolvedKeybindingsConfig } from "@t3tools/contracts"

// Used for shortcut matching and command resolution
```

**session-logic.ts** - Session orchestration:
```typescript
import { EventId, MessageId, TurnId, type OrchestrationThreadActivity } from "@t3tools/contracts"

// Used for event handling and thread state management
```

#### **Import Patterns Across 40+ Files in apps/web:**
- **~50 files import from `@t3tools/contracts`** (types, IDs, constants)
- **~3-4 files import from `@t3tools/shared/model`** (utility functions)
- **No imports** from `/git`, `/logging`, `/shell`, or `/Net` modules in web (those are server/backend utilities)
- **Branded ID types** (`ThreadId`, `ProjectId`, etc.) are imported in utility files like `lib/utils.ts` for type safety

---

### 6. KEY INSIGHTS & ARCHITECTURAL PATTERNS

**Type Safety Strategy:**
- Contracts package: All runtime-validated schemas using Effect.Schema
- Shared package: Pure utility functions that operate on contract types
- Web app: Imports types for compile-time safety, imports utilities for data transformation

**Separation of Concerns:**
- **contracts**: Domain types, RPC schemas, constants, interfaces
- **shared**: Pure algorithms and utilities (model resolution, git branch naming, logging, networking)
- **web**: UI logic, state management, component behavior

**Export Strategy:**
- contracts: Single default export (index.ts re-exports everything)
- shared: Multiple named subpath exports (no default export)

**Dependency Flow:**
- web → shared (utilities) → contracts (types)
- web → contracts (types and constants) → Effect

---

### Summary Files & Paths

**packages/shared:**
- `/packages/shared/package.json`
- `/packages/shared/src/model.ts` (79 lines)
- `/packages/shared/src/git.ts` (62 lines)
- `/packages/shared/src/logging.ts` (116 lines)
- `/packages/shared/src/shell.ts` (39 lines)
- `/packages/shared/src/Net.ts` (181 lines)

**packages/contracts:**
- `/packages/contracts/package.json`
- `/packages/contracts/src/index.ts` (main export)
- `/packages/contracts/src/baseSchemas.ts` (branded IDs)
- `/packages/contracts/src/model.ts` (model definitions)
- `/packages/contracts/src/orchestration.ts` (36KB - main domain logic)
- `/packages/contracts/src/provider.ts` (provider contracts)

- Plus: terminal, git, ipc, ws, server, keybindings, editor, project schemas

This structure enables type-safe, composable interactions across the monorepo while maintaining clear separation between domain contracts and utility implementations.

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the T3 Code web app. Let me compile my findings into a detailed report: ## T3 Code Web App - Thorough Exploration Report I've completed a comprehensive exploration of the T3 Code web app (`apps/web`). Here are my detailed findings: ### 1. **Directory Structure and File Organization** The app follows a clean, modular structure: - **`src/`** - Main application source code - **`routes/`** - TanStack Router file-based routing (`.tsx` files) - **`components/`** - React components (main components and `ui/` subdirectory) - **`hooks/`** - Custom React hooks (including `useTheme.ts`) - **`lib/`** - Utility libraries and helpers - **Core files**: `main.tsx`, `router.ts`, `store.ts`, `types.ts`, `index.css` - **Logic files**: `session-logic.ts`, `terminalStateStore.ts`, `composerDraftStore.ts`, `appSettings.ts`, etc. ### 2. **Bootstrapping & App Entry** **`index.html`:** - Simple HTML template with root div `
` - Loads DM Sans font from Google Fonts - Imports main.tsx as ES module **`src/main.tsx`:** ```tsx // Uses TanStack Router with context-based history // Creates hash history for Electron, browser history for web // Renders RouterProvider with StoreProvider wrap // Applies xterm.css and index.css globally ``` **Router Setup (`src/router.ts`):** - Creates QueryClient and passes to router context - Uses StoreProvider as Wrap component for context provision - File-based routing via `routeTree.gen.ts` (generated by TanStack Router plugin) **Root Route (`src/routes/__root.tsx`):** - Provides `ToastProvider` and `AnchoredToastProvider` - Wraps with `EventRouter` component for server state sync - Handles loading state during native API connection ### 3. **CSS/Styling Approach** **Modern Tailwind 4 with CSS Custom Properties:** **`src/index.css`:** - **Framework**: `@import "tailwindcss"` (Tailwind 4.0.0 with Vite plugin) - **Custom Theme Variables**: Extensive use of CSS custom properties (--color-*, --radius-*, etc.) - **Dark Mode**: - Uses `@custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark, .dark *))` for dark mode selector - Applied via `.dark` class on document root (set by `useTheme.ts`) - **Color System**: oklch color space for primary colors, color-mix() for dynamic colors - **Animations**: Custom skeleton animation defined in `@theme inline` - **Global Styles**: - Disables scrollbars for certain containers - Custom scrollbar styling (webkit) - Special handling for drag regions (Electron frameless window) - Chat markdown styling with custom rules **Component Styling:** - Uses `class-variance-authority` (CVA) for variant-based component styling - Tailwind merge for safe class merging - Example: `src/components/ui/button.tsx` uses CVA with multiple size/variant combinations - All components use semantic color variables (`text-foreground`, `bg-background`, `border-border`, etc.) ### 4. **React Context Providers & Hooks Patterns** **Providers Structure:** ``` RouterProvider (TanStack Router) → QueryClientProvider (React Query) → StoreProvider (custom Zustand store) → ToastProvider → AnchoredToastProvider ``` **Store Pattern (`src/store.ts`):** - Uses **Zustand** for client state management - `useStore` hook provides app state (projects, threads, session info) - `StoreProvider` component initializes state on mount - Persists state to localStorage on changes via `subscribe()` - Key state: - `projects`: Project list with expanded/collapsed state - `threads`: Chat threads with messages, models, session info - `threadsHydrated`: Sync flag - Methods: `syncServerReadModel`, `markThreadVisited`, `toggleProject`, etc. **Hook Patterns:** - **External Store Subscription**: Uses `useSyncExternalStore` for external state (theme, settings) - Custom hooks in `src/hooks/`: - `useTheme.ts` - Theme management - `useMediaQuery.ts` - Media query matching - `useTurnDiffSummaries.ts` - Diff summary access **AppSettings Hook (`src/appSettings.ts`):** - Also uses `useSyncExternalStore` pattern - Manages user preferences (model paths, service tier, custom models) - Validates/normalizes settings with Effect/Schema - Multi-tab sync via storage events ### 5. **localStorage Usage** **Two Primary Storage Keys:** 1. **`"t3code:theme"`** (in `useTheme.ts`): - Stores: `"light" | "dark" | "system"` - Default: `"system"` (respects OS preference) - Used for theme persistence across sessions 2. **`"t3code:app-settings:v1"`** (in `appSettings.ts`): - Stores JSON-serialized app settings - Fields: - `codexBinaryPath`: Custom Codex binary location - `codexHomePath`: Custom CODEX_HOME directory - `confirmThreadDelete`: Boolean flag - `enableAssistantStreaming`: Boolean flag - `codexServiceTier`: "auto" | "fast" | "flex" - `customCodexModels`: Array of custom model slugs 3. **`"t3code:renderer-state:v8"`** (in `store.ts`): - Stores project expanded state - Structure: `{ expandedProjectCwds: string[] }` - Syncs across tabs via storage events **Legacy Keys** (cleaned up automatically): - Multiple v1-v7 versions of settings and state keys **Storage Pattern:** - All use `window.localStorage.getItem()` / `setItem()` - All implement try/catch to gracefully handle quota errors - Use external store subscription for cross-tab sync - Implement immediate on-module-load reads to prevent flashing ### 6. **Existing Theme-Related Code** **`src/hooks/useTheme.ts`:** ```tsx // Full implementation: - type Theme = "light" | "dark" | "system" - getStored(): Reads from localStorage - applyTheme(): Adds/removes "dark" class on document root - Suppresses transitions during theme changes for UX - Listens to system preference changes (matchMedia) - Listens to storage events (multi-tab sync) - Returns: { theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme } ``` **Theme Application:** - Theme class applied to `document.documentElement` - CSS custom properties respond to `.dark` selector - System preference tracking via `window.matchMedia("(prefers-color-scheme: dark)")` - Immediate application on module load to prevent flash **Settings Usage:** - Theme selector in `src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` - Displays 3 options: System, Light, Dark - Shows "Active theme" indicator (resolved light/dark) - Integration with settings route UI ### 7. **Key Files Summary** | File | Purpose | |------|---------| | `index.html` | Entry point template | | `src/main.tsx` | App initialization & rendering | | `src/router.ts` | Router configuration with providers | | `src/store.ts` | Zustand store & persistence | | `src/appSettings.ts` | User preferences management | | `src/index.css` | Tailwind config & global styles | | `src/hooks/useTheme.ts` | Theme state management | | `src/routes/__root.tsx` | Root layout & event handling | | `src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | Settings page with theme selector | | `vite.config.ts` | Vite + Tailwind 4 setup | | `tsconfig.json` | TypeScript with path aliases (`~/`) | ### 8. **Important Patterns & Conventions** - Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the T3 Code web app. Let me compile my findings into a detailed report: ## T3 Code Web App - Thorough Exploration Report I've completed a comprehensive exploration of the T3 Code web app (`apps/web`). Here are my detailed findings: ### 1. **Directory Structure and File Organization** The app follows a clean, modular structure: - **`src/`** - Main application source code - **`routes/`** - TanStack Router file-based routing (`.tsx` files) - **`components/`** - React components (main components and `ui/` subdirectory) - **`hooks/`** - Custom React hooks (including `useTheme.ts`) - **`lib/`** - Utility libraries and helpers - **Core files**: `main.tsx`, `router.ts`, `store.ts`, `types.ts`, `index.css` - **Logic files**: `session-logic.ts`, `terminalStateStore.ts`, `composerDraftStore.ts`, `appSettings.ts`, etc. ### 2. **Bootstrapping & App Entry** **`index.html`:** - Simple HTML template with root div `
` - Loads DM Sans font from Google Fonts - Imports main.tsx as ES module **`src/main.tsx`:** ```tsx // Uses TanStack Router with context-based history // Creates hash history for Electron, browser history for web // Renders RouterProvider with StoreProvider wrap // Applies xterm.css and index.css globally ``` **Router Setup (`src/router.ts`):** - Creates QueryClient and passes to router context - Uses StoreProvider as Wrap component for context provision - File-based routing via `routeTree.gen.ts` (generated by TanStack Router plugin) **Root Route (`src/routes/__root.tsx`):** - Provides `ToastProvider` and `AnchoredToastProvider` - Wraps with `EventRouter` component for server state sync - Handles loading state during native API connection ### 3. **CSS/Styling Approach** **Modern Tailwind 4 with CSS Custom Properties:** **`src/index.css`:** - **Framework**: `@import "tailwindcss"` (Tailwind 4.0.0 with Vite plugin) - **Custom Theme Variables**: Extensive use of CSS custom properties (--color-*, --radius-*, etc.) - **Dark Mode**: - Uses `@custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark, .dark *))` for dark mode selector - Applied via `.dark` class on document root (set by `useTheme.ts`) - **Color System**: oklch color space for primary colors, color-mix() for dynamic colors - **Animations**: Custom skeleton animation defined in `@theme inline` - **Global Styles**: - Disables scrollbars for certain containers - Custom scrollbar styling (webkit) - Special handling for drag regions (Electron frameless window) - Chat markdown styling with custom rules **Component Styling:** - Uses `class-variance-authority` (CVA) for variant-based component styling - Tailwind merge for safe class merging - Example: `src/components/ui/button.tsx` uses CVA with multiple size/variant combinations - All components use semantic color variables (`text-foreground`, `bg-background`, `border-border`, etc.) ### 4. **React Context Providers & Hooks Patterns** **Providers Structure:** ``` RouterProvider (TanStack Router) → QueryClientProvider (React Query) → StoreProvider (custom Zustand store) → ToastProvider → AnchoredToastProvider ``` **Store Pattern (`src/store.ts`):** - Uses **Zustand** for client state management - `useStore` hook provides app state (projects, threads, session info) - `StoreProvider` component initializes state on mount - Persists state to localStorage on changes via `subscribe()` - Key state: - `projects`: Project list with expanded/collapsed state - `threads`: Chat threads with messages, models, session info - `threadsHydrated`: Sync flag - Methods: `syncServerReadModel`, `markThreadVisited`, `toggleProject`, etc. **Hook Patterns:** - **External Store Subscription**: Uses `useSyncExternalStore` for external state (theme, settings) - Custom hooks in `src/hooks/`: - `useTheme.ts` - Theme management - `useMediaQuery.ts` - Media query matching - `useTurnDiffSummaries.ts` - Diff summary access **AppSettings Hook (`src/appSettings.ts`):** - Also uses `useSyncExternalStore` pattern - Manages user preferences (model paths, service tier, custom models) - Validates/normalizes settings with Effect/Schema - Multi-tab sync via storage events ### 5. **localStorage Usage** **Two Primary Storage Keys:** 1. **`"t3code:theme"`** (in `useTheme.ts`): - Stores: `"light" | "dark" | "system"` - Default: `"system"` (respects OS preference) - Used for theme persistence across sessions 2. **`"t3code:app-settings:v1"`** (in `appSettings.ts`): - Stores JSON-serialized app settings - Fields: - `codexBinaryPath`: Custom Codex binary location - `codexHomePath`: Custom CODEX_HOME directory - `confirmThreadDelete`: Boolean flag - `enableAssistantStreaming`: Boolean flag - `codexServiceTier`: "auto" | "fast" | "flex" - `customCodexModels`: Array of custom model slugs 3. **`"t3code:renderer-state:v8"`** (in `store.ts`): - Stores project expanded state - Structure: `{ expandedProjectCwds: string[] }` - Syncs across tabs via storage events **Legacy Keys** (cleaned up automatically): - Multiple v1-v7 versions of settings and state keys **Storage Pattern:** - All use `window.localStorage.getItem()` / `setItem()` - All implement try/catch to gracefully handle quota errors - Use external store subscription for cross-tab sync - Implement immediate on-module-load reads to prevent flashing ### 6. **Existing Theme-Related Code** **`src/hooks/useTheme.ts`:** ```tsx // Full implementation: - type Theme = "light" | "dark" | "system" - getStored(): Reads from localStorage - applyTheme(): Adds/removes "dark" class on document root - Suppresses transitions during theme changes for UX - Listens to system preference changes (matchMedia) - Listens to storage events (multi-tab sync) - Returns: { theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme } ``` **Theme Application:** - Theme class applied to `document.documentElement` - CSS custom properties respond to `.dark` selector - System preference tracking via `window.matchMedia("(prefers-color-scheme: dark)")` - Immediate application on module load to prevent flash **Settings Usage:** - Theme selector in `src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` - Displays 3 options: System, Light, Dark - Shows "Active theme" indicator (resolved light/dark) - Integration with settings route UI ### 7. **Key Files Summary** | File | Purpose | |------|---------| | `index.html` | Entry point template | | `src/main.tsx` | App initialization & rendering | | `src/router.ts` | Router configuration with providers | | `src/store.ts` | Zustand store & persistence | | `src/appSettings.ts` | User preferences management | | `src/index.css` | Tailwind config & global styles | | `src/hooks/useTheme.ts` | Theme state management | | `src/routes/__root.tsx` | Root layout & event handling | | `src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | Settings page with theme selector | | `vite.config.ts` | Vite + Tailwind 4 setup | | `tsconfig.json` | TypeScript with path aliases (`~/`) | ### 8. **Important Patterns & Conventions** - **Path Aliases**: `~/` resolves to `src/` directory - **Component Slots**: Components use `data-slot` attributes for semantic structure - **Responsive Design**: Heavy use of Tailwind responsive prefixes (`sm:`) - **Accessibility**: Uses semantic HTML, ARIA attributes, focus management - **Type Safety**: Full TypeScript with Effect/Schema validation - **React Compiler**: Enabled via Babel plugin for optimization - **External Store Pattern**: Prefers `useSyncExternalStore` for non-React state - **Multi-Tab Sync**: Storage events monitored for real-time synchronization This comprehensive setup provides a solid foundation for implementing additional features like user preferences storage, theme customization, or enhanced settings management.

## Key Findings:

- Still need to persist to localStorage

Options:
1. **Replace**: Remove the old light/dark/system mode system, replace entirely with named themes
2. **Extend**: Keep the old system for dark/light mode, add named themes as an additional layer
3. **Merge**: Each named theme IS a light or dark theme (e.g., "t3-light", "t3-dark", "t3-midnight" are distinct themes that are either light or dark variants)

Given the task says "at least 3 built-in themes ('t3-dark', 't3-light', 't3-midnight')" - these appear to be standalone themes replacing the light/dark mode toggle entirely. The existing `useTheme` hook needs to be updated to support these named themes instead of just light/dark/system preferences. I'll structure this with a `ThemeDefinition` type that includes an id, label, isDark flag, and color tokens for each theme, then define the three built-in themes as instances of this type. Now I'm looking at the color tokens that need to be defined for each theme area. The current CSS variables cover background and foreground, but I need to add separate tokens for sidebar, editor, terminal, and status bar. I'll create new CSS custom properties like `--theme-sidebar-bg` and `--theme-editor-bg` that the theme system can set dynamically, so components can reference them without needing build config changes. Defining the interface structure...

Now I'm thinking through the built-in theme options—a light theme that mirrors the current light mode, a dark theme matching the existing dark mode, and a midnight theme with a deeper blue-black aesthetic. For the `useTheme` hook, I need to decide whether to preserve the current API shape that returns theme, setTheme, and resolvedTheme, or redesign it to support the new token-based system while keeping both the existing hook file and the settings component in sync.

The settings page currently displays System/Light/Dark radio options and uses the theme value to track selection, so updating it to show the named themes instead means I'll need to modify both the hook and the settings component together. I'm also considering the storage strategy—using a new key like `"t3code:color-theme"` to avoid conflicts with the existing `"t3code:theme"` key, though I need to think through whether to migrate or deprecate the old key. I'm mapping the old theme values to the new system: "system" defaults to "t3-dark", "light" becomes "t3-light", and "dark" becomes "t3-dark". The task asks for a theme provider, but the codebase already uses `useSyncExternalStore` with module-level state as its pattern, so I need to decide whether to stick with that established approach or add a React Context wrapper. I'll stick with the established pattern of `useSyncExternalStore` for the hook, and create a `ThemeProvider` component that applies theme CSS variables on mount to prevent flash. The `ThemeSelector` component will display color swatches for each theme option and handle selection, placed in the settings area.

Now I'm mapping out the specific files I need to create and modify: the theme definitions and types go in a shared package, the selector component in the web app, and I'll need to update the hook, root layout, settings page, and CSS to wire everything together.

I'm reconsidering whether to add a ThemeProvider to the root layout—the current pattern of running `applyTheme()` at module load time already prevents flash, and using `useSyncExternalStore` means I don't strictly need a provider. I could keep the lightweight approach where the hook handles initialization, but I'll still create a ThemeProvider as a backup to ensure the theme applies on mount and handles any edge cases.

Now I'm mapping out the implementation: first I'll create the theme definitions file with the color tokens, theme objects, and helper functions, then update the package exports, and finally rewrite the useTheme hook to use these definitions. from `@t3tools/shared/theme`
- Storage key: keep `"t3code:theme"` but handle old values via migration

- `applyTheme(themeId)` now:

  - Toggles `.dark` class based on `theme.isDark`

**Step 4: Create `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx`**
- Displays a grid of theme swatches
- Each swatch shows the theme's color palette visually
- Click to select

**Step 5: Update `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`**

**Step 6: Add new CSS variables to `apps/web/src/index.css`**

- Add these to the `@theme inline` block so Tailwind can use them

**Step 7: Add ThemeProvider to `apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx`**
- The existing hook already works without a provider using module-level state and `useSyncExternalStore`, but I should still create a `ThemeProvider` component that wraps children to handle theme initialization and persistence properly.
- I'll make it a simple wrapper that re-applies the theme on mount to catch any SSR or hydration edge cases, and expose the theme context for consistency with the provider pattern.
- The context will carry the full `ThemeDefinition` object so components can access the complete theme data.

Now I'm thinking through how to actually apply the theme colors to the DOM. I could either set CSS variables directly via JavaScript for each color token, which would override the stylesheet values and work with all existing Tailwind utilities, or I could toggle a class and rely on CSS variants. The direct approach is cleaner since it avoids conflicts between the class toggle and the CSS variables, though I need to be careful about which values get overridden.

Looking at the different ways to structure this, I could use theme-specific CSS classes, but that requires adding rules for every theme and loses the ability to support dynamic themes. A data attribute approach is cleaner and more modern—I'd set `data-theme="t3-dark"` on the HTML element and define CSS rules that target it, which avoids the need for runtime property setting while keeping the CSS declarative.

But I need to consider what's already in place. The existing system uses a `.dark` class that toggles based on `theme.isDark`, and there are Tailwind dark utilities and `@variant dark` CSS rules that depend on it. If I layer in a new data-attribute system, both would coexist, which adds complexity.

Actually, I think the cleanest path is to go all-in on setting CSS custom properties via JavaScript for every theme token. That way I can eliminate the need for `@variant dark` entirely—the CSS just declares the variable names, and JavaScript handles setting the actual values based on the selected theme. This gives me maximum flexibility without the overhead of maintaining multiple CSS rule sets.

The `.dark` class would still be needed for Tailwind's `dark:` utilities and any existing `@variant dark` CSS rules, but if I'm replacing the color system with JS-based theme application, I could eventually remove those CSS overrides. For now, the pragmatic approach is to set the CSS variables and let Tailwind pick them up naturally, keeping the `.dark` class toggle for backward compatibility. matic approach:
- Keep the `@variant dark` CSS block as a fallback
- The new theme system sets CSS variables via `style.setProperty()` which OVERRIDES the CSS file values
- Still toggle `.dark` class for Tailwind dark utilities compatibility
- The `@variant dark` block won't conflict because `style.setProperty()` creates inline styles which have higher specificity

This means:
- `t3-light` and `t3-dark` themes will have `.dark` class or not based on `isDark`
- `t3-midnight` (a dark theme) will have `.dark` class + its own specific colors applied via inline styles

**t3-light** (light theme, `isDark: false`):

**t3-dark** (dark theme, `isDark: true`):

- Editor: background
- Terminal: slightly lighter
- Status bar: dark border color

**t3-midnight** (deep dark, `isDark: true`):

- Primary/Accent: vibrant blue-purple
- Sidebar and editor progressively darker, terminal stays dark, status bar nearly black with blue tint

Now I'm defining the `ThemeColorTokens` type by mapping the key CSS variables from the existing system into camelCase properties, including the area-specific ones for sidebar, editor, terminal, and status bar, plus handling variables that use `color-mix()` functions.

For the `ThemeProvider` component, I'll create a React Context that manages the theme state and provides it through a hook. I should keep the existing `useTheme` hook in the hooks directory and create a new `ThemeProvider` component that wraps the app and applies the theme on mount, then integrate it into the root layout.

Now I'm mapping out the full implementation strategy: the existing `useTheme` hook already uses `useSyncExternalStore` with localStorage, the settings page has the UI controls for theme switching, and CSS variables handle the visual changes. I need to create the `ThemeProvider` component to tie these together, then add it to the root layout so the theme persists across the app. I've mapped out the implementation steps, and now I'm reviewing the root layout structure to understand how to integrate the ThemeProvider—once I see how the current setup works, I can finalize the complete plan.

## Custom Theme System — Implementation Plan

Here's exactly what will be built across **8 files** (3 new, 5 modified), with zero new dependencies or build config changes:

### New Files
1. **`packages/shared/src/theme.ts`** — `ThemeColorTokens` interface, `ThemeDefinition` type, and the 3 built-in themes as constants: `T3_DARK_THEME`, `T3_LIGHT_THEME`, `T3_MIDNIGHT_THEME`. Includes `BUILT_IN_THEMES` array, `getThemeById()` helper, and a `themeColorsToCssVars()` mapping utility.

2. **`apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx`** — React Context wrapper. Internally calls `useTheme()` (which uses `useSyncExternalStore`, the established codebase pattern) and provides the resolved `ThemeDefinition` via context. Added to `__root.tsx`.

3. **`apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx`** — Visual swatch grid showing all 3 themes. Each swatch renders color strips using inline styles drawn from the theme's color tokens. Click to select; shows a "Selected" badge on the active theme. Accessible (`role="radiogroup"`, `aria-checked`).

### Modified Files
4. **`packages/shared/package.json`** — Add `"./theme"` subpath export pointing to the new `theme.ts`.

5. **`apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`** — Full rewrite. New storage key `"t3code:color-theme"` (with migration from old `"t3code:theme"` values: `"light"→"t3-light"`, others→`"t3-dark"`). The `applyTheme()` function now sets all CSS custom properties via `style.setProperty()` for the chosen theme, toggles `.dark` class based on `theme.isDark`, and sets `color-scheme`. Module-load-time flash prevention is preserved.

6. **`apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx`** — Wrap `RootRouteView` return with `<ThemeProvider>`.

7. **`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`** — Replace the Appearance section's manual radio group with `<ThemeSelector />`.

content.text:
Here's a summary of the complete plan:

---

## Plan: Custom Theme System for T3 Code

**8 files total — 3 new, 5 modified. Zero new dependencies.**

### New Files

1. **`packages/shared/src/theme.ts`** — Defines `ThemeColorTokens` (19 core tokens + 6 area-specific: sidebar, editor, terminal, statusbar), `ThemeDefinition`, and the 3 built-in themes as constants. Exports `BUILT_IN_THEMES`, `getThemeById()`, and a `themeColorsToCssVars()` helper. Pure TypeScript, no Effect needed.

2. **`apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx`** — React Context wrapping `useTheme()`. Thin provider that exposes `{ themeId, theme, setTheme }` to the component tree. Added to both branches of `RootRouteView` in `__root.tsx`.

3. **`apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx`** — A visual swatch grid. Each theme gets a mini color preview rendered with inline styles (background strip, sidebar strip, accent/primary pip, statusbar). Fully accessible (`role="radiogroup"`, `aria-checked`). Reuses the existing "Selected" badge style from the current settings radio buttons.

### Modified Files

4. **`packages/shared/package.json`** — Add `"./theme"` subpath export.

5. **`apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`** — Full rewrite retaining the `useSyncExternalStore` + module-level-state pattern. New storage key `"t3code:color-theme"` with migration from the old `"t3code:theme"` format (`"light"→"t3-light"`, others→`"t3-dark"`). The `applyTheme()` function now sets all CSS vars via `style.setProperty()`, toggles `.dark` for Tailwind compatibility, and sets `color-scheme`.

6. **`apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx`** — Wrap with `<ThemeProvider>`.

7. **`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`** — Replace the manual radio group in the Appearance section with `<ThemeSelector />`.

8. **`apps/web/src/index.css`** — Add fallback values for `--sidebar-background`, `--editor-background`, `--terminal-background`, `--statusbar-background` (and foreground variants) in both `:root` and `@variant dark`, plus register them in `@theme inline` for Tailwind utility access.

tool_input.summary:
## Custom Theme System — Implementation Plan

Here's exactly what will be built across **8 files** (3 new, 5 modified), with zero new dependencies or build config changes:

### New Files
1. **`packages/shared/src/theme.ts`** — `ThemeColorTokens` interface, `ThemeDefinition` type, and the 3 built-in themes as constants: `T3_DARK_THEME`, `T3_LIGHT_THEME`, `T3_MIDNIGHT_THEME`. Includes `BUILT_IN_THEMES` array, `getThemeById()` helper, and a `themeColorsToCssVars()` mapping utility.

2. **`apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx`** — React Context wrapper. Internally calls `useTheme()` (which uses `useSyncExternalStore`, the established codebase pattern) and provides the resolved `ThemeDefinition` via context. Added to `__root.tsx`.

3. **`apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx`** — Visual swatch grid showing all 3 themes. Each swatch renders color strips using inline styles drawn from the theme's color tokens. Click to select; shows a "Selected" badge on the active theme. Accessible (`role="radiogroup"`, `aria-checked`).

### Modified Files
4. **`packages/shared/package.json`** — Add `"./theme"` subpath export pointing to the new `theme.ts`.

5. **`apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`** — Full rewrite. New storage key `"t3code:color-theme"` (with migration from old `"t3code:theme"` values: `"light"→"t3-light"`, others→`"t3-dark"`). The `applyTheme()` function now sets all CSS custom properties via `style.setProperty()` for the chosen theme, toggles `.dark` class based on `theme.isDark`, and sets `color-scheme`. Module-load-time flash prevention is preserved.

6. **`apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx`** — Wrap `RootRouteView` return with `<ThemeProvider>`.

7. **`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`** — Replace the Appearance section's manual radio group with `<ThemeSelector />`.

8. **`apps/web/src/index.css`** — Add CSS variable declarations for the 6 new area-specific tokens (`--sidebar-background`, `--sidebar-foreground`, `--editor-background`, `--terminal-background`, `--statusbar-background`, `--statusbar-foreground`) in both light and dark `:root` blocks, and register them in `@theme inline` for Tailwind utility access.

5	## Plan: Custom Theme System for T3 Code
6	
7	**8 files total — 3 new, 5 modified. Zero new dependencies.**
8	
9	### New Files
10	
11	1. **`packages/shared/src/theme.ts`** — Defines `ThemeColorTokens` (19 core tokens + 6 area-specific: sidebar, editor, terminal, statusbar), `ThemeDefinition`, and the 3 built-in themes as constants. Exports `BUILT_IN_THEMES`, `getThemeById()`, and a `themeColorsToCssVars()` helper. Pure TypeScript, no Effect needed.
12	
13	2. **`apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx`** — React Context wrapping `useTheme()`. Thin provider that exposes `{ themeId, theme, setTheme }` to the component tree. Added to both branches of `RootRouteView` in `__root.tsx`.

Each theme gets a mini color preview rendered with inline styles (background strip, sidebar strip, accent/primary pip, statusbar). Fully accessible (`role="radiogroup"`, `aria-checked`). Reuses the existing "Selected" badge style from the current settings radio buttons.

New storage key `"t3code:color-theme"` with migration from the old `"t3code:theme"` format (`"light"→"t3-light"`, others→`"t3-dark"`). The `applyTheme()` function now sets all CSS vars via `style.setProperty()`, toggles `.dark` for Tailwind compatibility, and sets `color-scheme`.

file.content:
Here's a summary of the complete plan:

---

## Plan: Custom Theme System for T3 Code

**8 files total — 3 new, 5 modified. Zero new dependencies.**

### New Files

1. **`packages/shared/src/theme.ts`** — Defines `ThemeColorTokens` (19 core tokens + 6 area-specific: sidebar, editor, terminal, statusbar), `ThemeDefinition`, and the 3 built-in themes as constants. Exports `BUILT_IN_THEMES`, `getThemeById()`, and a `themeColorsToCssVars()` helper. Pure TypeScript, no Effect needed.

2. **`apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx`** — React Context wrapping `useTheme()`. Thin provider that exposes `{ themeId, theme, setTheme }` to the component tree. Added to both branches of `RootRouteView` in `__root.tsx`.

3. **`apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx`** — A visual swatch grid. Each theme gets a mini color preview rendered with inline styles (background strip, sidebar strip, accent/primary pip, statusbar). Fully accessible (`role="radiogroup"`, `aria-checked`). Reuses the existing "Selected" badge style from the current settings radio buttons.

### Modified Files

4. **`packages/shared/package.json`** — Add `"./theme"` subpath export.

5. **`apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`** — Full rewrite retaining the `useSyncExternalStore` + module-level-state pattern. New storage key `"t3code:color-theme"` with migration from the old `"t3code:theme"` format (`"light"→"t3-light"`, others→`"t3-dark"`). The `applyTheme()` function now sets all CSS vars via `style.setProperty()`, toggles `.dark` for Tailwind compatibility, and sets `color-scheme`.

6. **`apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx`** — Wrap with `<ThemeProvider>`.

7. **`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`** — Replace the manual radio group in the Appearance section with `<ThemeSelector />`.

8. **`apps/web/src/index.css`** — Add fallback values for `--sidebar-background`, `--editor-background`, `--terminal-background`, `--statusbar-background` (and foreground variants) in both `:root` and `@variant dark`, plus register them in `@theme inline` for Tailwind utility access.

The migration needs to map the old storage key `"t3code:theme"` to the new `"t3code:color-theme"` key, converting the old format to the new theme names.

For the `useTheme` hook rewrite, I need to keep the `useSyncExternalStore` pattern but update it to work with theme IDs instead of the old system, apply CSS custom properties directly instead of toggling classes, and handle the storage migration. The ThemeProvider will wrap the app and manage the theme state while the hook provides access to it. I'm realizing the settings page currently imports `useTheme` directly, but according to the plan I should create a `ThemeSelector` component that uses the context instead. The cleanest approach would be to have `useTheme.ts` handle the store logic with `useSyncExternalStore` for persistence and DOM updates, while `ThemeProvider.tsx` wraps the app and exposes that through context. logic (localStorage + CSS vars + `useSyncExternalStore`)
- `ThemeProvider.tsx` creates a React context that uses `useTheme` internally and exposes values
- `ThemeSelector.tsx` consumes the context via `useTheme()` or similar
- The settings page imports `ThemeSelector` and drops the old radio group

So the cleaner path is: keep `useTheme.ts` as the store-level hook, have `ThemeProvider.tsx` wrap it in context, then use `ThemeSelector.tsx` to consume that context. The settings page swaps out the manual radio group for the `ThemeSelector` component.

Looking at the PLAN again, it's clear the intent is to use React Context wrapping `useTheme()` with a thin provider that exposes theme state to the component tree. So I'll structure it as: `useTheme.ts` handles the module-level store and `useSyncExternalStore` hook plus DOM initialization, while `ThemeProvider.tsx` creates the context layer on top and exports the necessary values. I'm defining the core color token interface that will hold all the theme values—the basic palette like background and foreground colors, plus semantic tokens for different UI areas like the sidebar and editor, along with a color scheme indicator.

Now I'm setting up the mapping between these token names and their corresponding CSS variable names... Now I'm exporting the three built-in theme definitions and setting up a theme lookup function that defaults to the dark theme if an ID isn't found. Then I'm moving into the hook layer where I'll use `useSyncExternalStore` to manage theme state, handle localStorage persistence with migration support, and apply the theme by updating CSS variables, the dark class, and color-scheme property. Writing theme application logic... Writing the hook implementation...

I'm realizing the snapshot should just be the theme ID string for simplicity, since `useSyncExternalStore` will handle identity comparison fine with primitives. The hook returns the theme ID, the full theme object, and a setter that updates localStorage and applies the theme with a transition suppression flag.

Now I'm setting up the ThemeProvider component that wraps the `useTheme` hook and exposes it through context, along with a custom hook to access that context safely with an error boundary check.

Building the theme selector component... Writing the theme preview card... Still writing CSS variables... Still writing theme variables...

on:

content.text:
All 8 steps are complete. Here's a summary of every change made:

---

## What was implemented

### New files (3)

**`packages/shared/src/theme.ts`** — Pure TypeScript theme definitions with no runtime dependencies:
- `ThemeColorTokens` interface — 19 core tokens (`background`, `foreground`, `primary`, `card`, `muted`, `border`, etc.) + 6 area-specific tokens (`sidebarBackground/Foreground`, `editorBackground`, `terminalBackground`, `statusbarBackground/Foreground`)
- `ThemeDefinition` — typed `{ id, label, description, colors }`
- `themeColorsToCssVars()` — maps token keys to CSS property names (`cardForeground` → `--card-foreground`, `colorScheme` → `color-scheme`, etc.)
- Three built-in themes: **T3 Dark** (charcoal), **T3 Light** (bright), **T3 Midnight** (deep navy/slate)
- `BUILT_IN_THEMES`, `DEFAULT_THEME_ID`, `getThemeById()` helpers

**`apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx`** — Thin React context that calls `useTheme()` internally and exposes `{ themeId, theme, setTheme }` via `useThemeContext()`.

**`apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx`** — A responsive 3-column grid of theme cards. Each card renders a live colour-preview swatch built from the theme's own tokens (sidebar strip, status bar strip, accent bar, content lines) entirely with inline styles. Accessible `role="radiogroup"` / `role="radio"` / `aria-checked` pattern with a "Selected" badge matching the existing UI conventions.

### Modified files (5)

**`packages/shared/package.json`** — Added `"./theme"` subpath export so `@t3tools/shared/theme` resolves correctly.

**`apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`** — Full rewrite retaining `useSyncExternalStore` + module-level store pattern. New storage key `"t3code:color-theme"`, migration from legacy `"t3code:theme"` (`"light"→"t3-light"`, anything else→`"t3-dark"`). `applyTheme()` now iterates CSS var pairs from `themeColorsToCssVars()` and calls `style.setProperty()` for each, handles `color-scheme`, and toggles `.dark` for Tailwind compatibility.

**`apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx`** — Wrapped both branches of `RootRouteView` with `<ThemeProvider>`.

**`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`** — Replaced the manual 3-option radio group and "Active theme" line with `<ThemeSelector />`. Removed `THEME_OPTIONS`, `useTheme` import.

**`apps/web/src/index.css`** — Added 6 area-specific CSS vars (`--sidebar-background`, `--sidebar-foreground`, `--editor-background`, `--terminal-background`, `--statusbar-background`, `--statusbar-foreground`) with light/dark fallbacks in `:root` and `@variant dark`, plus registered them in `@theme inline` for Tailwind utility access.
